summary: Verified critical bug fix for COMPLEX_DISQUALIFIERS in isSimpleFrontendEdit. The word 'subscription' alone no longer disqualifies landing page requests from direct edit mode. All 12 test cases passed.

test_1_subscription_box_landing_page.input: Build me a landing page for Moonbean Coffee with pricing for a subscription box. Make it stunning.

test_2_subscription_box_plans.input: Build a pricing page with subscription box plans

test_3_bakery_homepage.input: Create a beautiful homepage for my bakery

test_4_stripe_integration.input: Build a page with stripe integration

test_5_payment_gateway.input: Build a checkout with payment gateway processing

test_6_auth_database.input: Build a dashboard with authentication system and database

test_7_landing_page.input: Build me a landing page for Moonbean Coffee

login_flow.details: Login successful via Supabase Auth (Enter key submission)

dashboard_load.details: Dashboard loads with prompt bar, project list, Growth button, model selectors (E-1, Ultra, Claude 4.5 Opus)

critical_code_review_comments: COMPLEX_DISQUALIFIERS at lines 437-444 now correctly uses specific patterns like 'stripe integration', 'payment gateway', 'checkout system' instead of broad 'subscription' match
isSimpleFrontendEdit correctly returns true for 'subscription box' marketing content while still blocking actual payment integrations
The fix ensures landing pages with pricing/subscription box content route to premium direct-edit path with quality instructions

success_rate.backend: 100% (all syntax checks and function tests passed)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 55 verified the critical COMPLEX_DISQUALIFIERS fix. The word 'subscription' alone no longer disqualifies requests from isSimpleFrontendEdit. Specific patterns like 'stripe integration', 'payment gateway processing', 'checkout system' still correctly disqualify. All 12 test cases passed including login and dashboard load.
